Patch Set 1:

I can't access MRP-2739, so can you describe what corruption will it cause for non-directory? Previously it's done for non-directory file is because we want to reuse obsolete dentry (lld_invalid is set) to avoid allocating new dentry, and such obsolete dentry exists because lustre client doesn't shrink them proactively, but let dcache to do it.

So I don't object to change this, but it's better to add code to reclaim obsolete dentries to avoid consuming too much memory.

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

> I can't access MRP-2739, so can you describe what corruption will
 > it cause for non-directory? Previously it's done for non-directory
 > file is because we want to reuse obsolete dentry (lld_invalid is
 > set) to avoid allocating new dentry, and such obsolete dentry
 > exists because lustre client doesn't shrink them proactively, but
 > let dcache to do it.
 > 
 > So I don't object to change this, but it's better to add code to
 > reclaim obsolete dentries to avoid consuming too much memory.

I am pasting here my vmcore analysis
Looking at the registers Parameter values are… 
victim->d_parent->d_inode = %RSI = ffff88009fa99440
inode = %RDI = ffff8800a2ef7b38
First parameter : victim->d_parent->d_inode
  crash> struct dentry ffff88009fa99440
struct dentry {
  d_count = { counter = 1 },
  d_mounted = 0 
  d_inode = 0xffff88009d954138, 
  d_parent = 0xffff88009cc818c0, 
  d_name = { name = 0xffff88009fa994e0 "12" }
}

crash> struct dentry 0xffff88009cc818c0
struct dentry {
  d_count = { counter = 15 }, 
  d_mounted = 0
  d_inode = 0xffff88009e0b91b8, <<<< This is not the same as inode from the comparison in the code
  d_parent = 0xffff8800af208240, 
  d_name = { name = 0xffff88009cc81960 "16" }
} 
Second Parameter
crash> inode.i_dentry ffff8800a2ef7b38
  i_dentry = {
    next = 0xffff8800af2082b0, 
    prev = 0xffff8800af2082b0
  }
crash> dentry -l dentry.d_alias 0xffff8800af2082b0
struct dentry {
  d_count = { counter = 1102 }, 
  d_mounted = 0
  d_inode = 0xffff8800a2ef7b38, 
  d_parent = 0xffff8800af208240, 
  d_name = {
    name = 0xffff8800af2082e0 "/" (ROOT)
  }
To continue up the dentry chain of the first parameter...
crash> struct dentry 0xffff8800af208240
struct dentry {
  d_count = {
    counter = 1102
  }, 
  d_inode = 0xffff8800a2ef7b38,    <<<<    This is indeed the second parameter i.e. inode 
  d_parent = 0xffff8800af208240 (ROOT)
  d_name = {
    name = 0xffff8800af2082e0 "/"
  }
So, inode is actually pointing to victim->d_parent->d_parent->d_inode instead of victim->d_parent->d_inode. 

Above corruption is because we were passing non-directories for ll_splice_alias

you can refer this link
https://www.kernel.org/doc/htmldocs/filesystems/API-d-splice-alias.html
as per this 
"This is needed in the lookup routine of any filesystem that is exportable (via knfsd) so that we can build dcache paths to directories effectively."

parents.subject: LU-4039 tests: enable test_90 for replay-single

commit.subject: LU-7613 llite: changes to avoid cache corruption
commit.message:
LU-7613 llite: changes to avoid cache corruption

ll_find_alias is responsible for getting alias for inode
which can be reused. Directories are assumed to have unique
alias, where in case of non-directories there can be multiple
aliases. In case of lustre there can be two type of aliases
i.e. discon_alias and invalid_alias. Usage of discon_alias in
case of non-directories may corrupt dcache and leads to kernel
crash. Changes made to avoid use of discon_alias in case of
non-directories.

Seagate-bug-id: MRP-2739, MRP-3601
Change-Id: Ieb9dabab0784bdb3c52e2cb32be1a766ffe55313
Signed-off-by: Lokesh Nagappa Jaliminche <lokesh.jaliminche@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Ujjwal Lanjewar <ujjwal.lanjewar@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Ashish Purkar <ashish.purkar@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Andrew Perepechko <andrew.perepechko@seagate.com>
Tested-by: Parinay Vijayprakash Kondekar <parinay.kondekar@seagate.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17732
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
